prompts: Conic sections are the curves derived from the intersection of a plane with a cone. An ellipse appears as an oval shape when the plane cuts through the cone at an angle, resulting in a closed curve. A parabola is formed when the plane is parallel to the cone's side and is represented as a U-shaped curve that can open either upwards or downwards. A hyperbola emerges when the plane cuts through both nappes of the cone, creating two separate, symmetrical curves that open away from each other, often resembling two mirrored, elongated U shapes.
